text: Modelling the chemical reactions in the interstellar medium (ISM) that make up the gas and dust is an area of interest for astrophysicists. Modelling is now transitioning from being qualitative to being quantitative based on new laboratory and theoretical studies. This enables measurements for individual reactions that more accurately represent the ISM and hence are more accurate rate constants and efficiency values are available. In general, the compositions in e.g. star-forming clouds are rarely in steady-state and this demands full integration of chemical modelling with dynamical modelling of the environment. This work aims to assess the status quo of chemical models applicable for use in dark clouds — the first stage in any stellar or planetary disk formation.
explanation: The passage reads like a polished, high-level abstract with generic statements and no specific data or citations. This boilerplate style is more typical of AI-generated text, though it could also be human-written.
